summary: Backend-only integration testing of the multi-account / proxy-browser feature. Added 16 pytest cases in /app/backend/tests/test_browsers_api.py covering /api/browsers CRUD + default-flag semantics, connect/disconnect/launch cloud behaviour, browser_id persistence on /api/watch and /api/drops, /api/status.browsers, legacy /api/brave/connect default-row sync, and regression on meta/profiles/settings/watch start-stop. All 16 new tests pass and all 39 pre-existing tests still pass (55/55 green).

minor.issue: API allows deleting the seeded Default row (returns 200). Per problem statement this is acceptable because UI enforces it, but a server-side guard + clearer error would be safer.

action_items: (Optional / low priority) Consider blocking DELETE of the is_default=1 browser row at the API layer and returning 400 with a clear message, instead of relying solely on UI guard.
(Optional) /api/browsers/{id}/connect currently returns 200 with connected:false and a reason on failure — good. Confirm the same contract is used by the frontend so the UI can show the error string.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 592 lines — approaching the 700-line split threshold; consider splitting routes into modules (browsers, watch, drops, profiles, settings) as the file keeps growing.

DELETE /api/browsers/{bid} does not prevent removing the last/default row. If both UI protections are bypassed (direct API), the default seed rule re-creates one only on next init_db, so a user could end up with zero browser rows and Watchlist/Drops referencing a deleted browser_id.

update_browser does not validate that is_default cannot be set to false on the only remaining default — toggling is_default:false on the sole default leaves the system with zero defaults. Not covered by this test since PATCH with is_default=false was not in scope, but worth a guard.
browsers/{id}/launch uses subprocess.Popen synchronously inside an async handler; tiny risk of event-loop blocking on slow FS. Low priority given it only runs on desktop.
_parse_cdp_port silently falls back to 9222 on any parse error — may mask user typos in cdp_url. Consider raising 400 on bad input.
No pagination / limit on /api/browsers, /api/watch, /api/drops — acceptable for a local scalper bot but worth noting.
seed_data_creation: Every test row is prefixed TEST_ and deleted in teardown. The auto-seeded Default browser row is preserved. The test that flips is_default restores it back to the original default before deleting the TEST row.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reusable pytest file /app/backend/tests/test_browsers_api.py exercises the full /api/browsers surface + browser_id propagation via the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. It is idempotent, cleans up its own rows, and should be run alongside the existing suite (`cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/ -q`). Brave is NOT available in the cloud, so connect returns connected:false with a reason and launch returns 404 — both expected and asserted.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. Feature works as specified across all 14 enumerated scenarios in the review request.
